title: Northlantic Insurance auto-insurance rate card: InDesign data-merge a supplied premium-tier CSV into the client's authored .indd, refresh the disclosure footer, and export a print-ready PDF + high-res JPEG
grounding_note: Grounded in the Freelancer.com candidate brief "Fast-Food Menu Illustrator Design", VERBATIM: the client has "a full brand style guide, so the colors, fonts, and overall layout imagery from that guide must come through clearly in the final piece"; the deliverable must "organize items, descriptions, and prices in a way that is easy to read at a glance"; the freelancer must "Prepare both an editable .AI file for future updates and a press-ready PDF for immediate printing"; and "Accuracy and attention to detail matter; I don't want to hunt for missing fonts or [mismatched data]." REASONABLY ADAPTED to the slot (category=datamerge_print, family=Layout & Data, output=document, workflow_nature=edit, vertical=finance_insurance): the real intent — take a fixed brand-locked template and bind a structured list of items/descriptions/prices into a press-ready-PDF-plus-editable-source deliverable — is the exact shape of an InDesign data merge, which IS in connector scope. The generic "restaurant menu" is reskinned as an insurance-broker RATE CARD (a price list / premium table), which is the finance-vertical analogue of a menu and the slot's own keyword set ("price list", "rate card", "menu", "data merge", "table"). The real brand (fast-food restaurant) is replaced by a fictional, IP-clean insurance brand, Northlantic Insurance Group, with its own brand kit. The client supplies the authored .indd rate-card template carrying real InDesign merge fields plus a CSV of premium tiers, exactly matching the brief's "editable source + press-ready PDF" and "on-brand, accurate data" requirements. No deliverable is invented beyond the price-list document the brief asks for; no from-scratch layout composition is performed by the connector (the .indd is a supplied authored input).
one_line_ask: Run the client's authored InDesign rate-card template through an InDesign data-merge against a supplied auto-insurance premium-tier CSV, place the on-brand logo and licensed background texture, refresh the compliance disclosure footer, and export a press-ready PDF plus a high-resolution JPEG.
full_brief: Northlantic Insurance Group, a regional personal-lines insurance broker, needs its quarterly Personal Auto rate card finished for print and for the branch counters. Our design lead has already built and handed off the authored InDesign template (auto_rate_card.indd) — it is fully brand-locked to our style guide (Northlantic Slate #1F2A44 and Signal Amber #F4A82C, headline face Basis Grotesque, body face Source Serif) and carries a single-page A4 portrait rate table with REAL InDesign data-merge fields (<<coverage_tier>>, <<coverage_desc>>, <<annual_premium>>, <<monthly_premium>>, <<deductible>>) laid out across four coverage bands (Liability Only, Standard, Standard Plus, Full Coverage), a logo placement frame, a full-bleed background frame, and a boxed disclosure/footer frame. We are NOT asking you to redesign the layout — the layout is locked. What we need is the production pass: (1) bind our supplied Q3 premium data (auto_premium_tiers.csv) into the template via a proper InDesign data merge so every tier, description, annual/monthly premium and deductible lands in the right cell — accuracy is critical, this is regulated pricing and one wrong number is a reprint; (2) place our brand wordmark crisply — vectorize the flat wordmark PNG we send so it prints razor-sharp at large size, and drop it in the logo frame; (3) back the card with a subtle premium paper/linen texture — license one from Adobe Stock (nothing loud, it must sit behind the table without hurting legibility) and set it as the background; (4) our compliance line changed this quarter — the small disclosure footer PNG we supply (new state-filing language + effective date) must be placed in the footer frame so the printed card carries the current legal text; (5) export the finished, merged document as a press-ready ~300 DPI A4 PDF for the printer AND a high-resolution JPEG for the intranet and the tablet quoting tool. Keep everything strictly on-brand and do not alter any premium value — bind exactly what the CSV says.

inputs.name: auto_rate_card.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a single-page A4 portrait Northlantic Insurance Personal Auto rate card, brand-locked to the Northlantic style guide (Northlantic Slate #1F2A44, Signal Amber #F4A82C, headline face Basis Grotesque, body face Source Serif). Must contain REAL InDesign data-merge placeholder fields named <<coverage_tier>>, <<coverage_desc>>, <<annual_premium>>, <<monthly_premium>>, <<deductible>> arranged as repeating rows under four coverage-band headers (Liability Only, Standard, Standard Plus, Full Coverage), plus a logo placement frame, a full-bleed background frame for a texture, and a boxed disclosure/footer frame. Authored by hand in InDesign so the merge fields actually bind; a PDF-converted .indd with literal text would NOT bind.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the client's own brand-locked template (a normal handoff / quarterly refresh). document_merge_data_layout is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields — it cannot be connector-generated, so it is a supplied input, not a chain output. Matches the brief's 'editable source + full brand style guide' requirement.

inputs.name: auto_premium_tiers.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns coverage_tier,coverage_desc,annual_premium,monthly_premium,deductible holding a Q3 personal-auto rate table: rows grouped under four bands — Liability Only, Standard, Standard Plus, Full Coverage — each with 1-3 sub-rows (e.g. by driver age band or region), a short one-line plain-English coverage description, an annual premium (e.g. $612.00), a monthly premium (e.g. $53.00) and a deductible (e.g. $500). About 10-12 data rows total. Realistic personal-auto premium figures. Column names must EXACTLY match the InDesign merge fields <<coverage_tier>>,<<coverage_desc>>,<<annual_premium>>,<<monthly_premium>>,<<deductible>>.
inputs.realism_notes: The structured premium data the merge binds; honors the brief's 'organize items, descriptions and prices' + 'accuracy matters, don't hunt for mismatched data'. Column headers match the authored .indd merge fields so the [T] data-merge binds cleanly.

inputs.name: northlantic_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat corporate insurance-broker wordmark reading 'Northlantic' with a small compass-rose / north-star glyph to the left, in a clean modern sans-serif, deep slate-blue letters (#1F2A44) with a single amber accent (#F4A82C) on the compass glyph, on a transparent background. Crisp flat raster PNG, high contrast, no gradients, no photographic effects, suitable for vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image for clean legible text/logo; flat high-contrast on transparent so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG that prints razor-sharp. IP-clean fictional brand.

inputs.name: northlantic_disclosure_footer.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A small flat rectangular footer graphic of dense legal disclosure text for an insurance rate card: fine-print paragraph reading like state-filing compliance language plus an effective date 'Rates effective Q3, filed with State Dept. of Insurance', in tiny dark-grey serif type on a plain light band, left-aligned, no logo, no color. Flat raster PNG, sharp, readable at small size.
inputs.realism_notes: Supplied as a flat image (not editable text) to model a compliance-approved artwork block that must be dropped in verbatim — the freelancer places it, does not retype it. Justifies a place-into-footer-frame step; kept as an image so no text authoring/typesetting is claimed.

inputs.name: northlantic_paper_linen_reference.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: Write a one-paragraph art-direction note from Northlantic's marketing lead specifying the desired background: 'a very subtle off-white premium paper / linen texture, low contrast, nothing loud — it must sit behind the rate table without reducing legibility; think cream stationery, faint fiber grain, no pattern motifs'. Include the search terms to use on Adobe Stock: 'subtle cream linen paper texture', 'off-white fine paper grain'.
inputs.realism_notes: A realistic client art-direction note that seeds the Adobe Stock search + license steps and constrains the texture to a legibility-safe choice; not itself an image asset.

outputs.name: Northlantic_Auto_RateCard_print.pdf
outputs.spec: Press-ready single-page A4 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; all premium tiers/descriptions/annual+monthly premiums/deductibles bound from auto_premium_tiers.csv, vectorized wordmark placed, licensed linen texture as background, current disclosure footer placed; ~300 DPI print quality with bleed.

outputs.name: Northlantic_Auto_RateCard_highres.jpg
outputs.spec: High-resolution JPEG of the same bound one-page rate card (A4 portrait, max-quality) for the intranet and the branch tablet quoting tool.

outputs.name: northlantic_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the Northlantic wordmark for crisp scaling at large print size, placed in the template's logo frame.

outputs.name: northlantic_linen_texture_fullres
outputs.spec: The licensed, full-resolution Adobe Stock cream/linen paper texture used as the rate card's full-bleed background.

connector_workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for a subtle cream/off-white linen paper texture using the marketing lead's art-direction search terms (low-res candidates).
License and download the chosen linen texture at full-res (license BEFORE use); becomes the rate card's full-bleed background asset.
Inline-preview the licensed texture to confirm it is subtle enough to sit behind the rate table without hurting legibility (the brief's legibility constraint).
Vectorize the flat Northlantic wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it prints razor-sharp at large size in the logo frame.
Crop the supplied disclosure-footer PNG to its content bounds so it drops cleanly into the template's boxed footer frame with no stray margin.
Initialize an upload session for all prepped placement assets (vector wordmark, trimmed disclosure footer, licensed linen texture).
Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference for the logo, footer and background frames.
Initialize a second upload session for the client's authored .indd template and the Q3 premium-tier CSV so the merge engine can reach them.
Finalize the template+CSV upload; returns the URNs the data-merge step consumes.
Create a Northlantic rate-card project folder in CC storage and organize the uploaded template, CSV and placement assets so the merge references a clean structure.

InDesign data-merge: bind the coverage_tier/coverage_desc/annual_premium/monthly_premium/deductible rows into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>); the vectorized wordmark, linen background and disclosure footer sit in their frames. [T] requires the user-authored template; binds exactly what the CSV says (no value altered).
Export the merged InDesign document to a press-ready ~300 DPI A4 portrait PDF with bleed — the printer deliverable.
Export the same merged document to a high-resolution max-quality JPEG for the intranet and the branch tablet quoting tool.
Inline-preview the exported print PDF as a proof so the bound premiums, placed logo, texture and disclosure can be eyeballed before sign-off (accuracy check per the brief).

chaining_note: Strict output→input chaining throughout. The background sub-chain runs asset_search(1) → license_and_download(2) → inline_preview(3), each consuming the prior output; the search is seeded by the supplied art-direction note. The wordmark chains raw PNG → vectorize(4). The disclosure footer chains raw PNG → crop_to_bounds(5). The three prepped placement assets (northlantic_wordmark.svg, disclosure_footer_trimmed, northlantic_linen_texture_fullres) converge into upload_session(6) → finalize(7) → uploaded_asset_urns. In parallel the authored .indd + CSV go through their own upload(8) → finalize(9) → template_data_urns. Both URN sets plus a fresh project folder(10) converge into the [T] data-merge(11), the single source for both exports — render PDF(12) and render JPEG(13). The PDF feeds a proof inline_preview(14). Every step after the two raw-asset seed points (n1 seeds from the supplied note; n4/n5/n8 seed from raw supplied inputs) is wired via inputs_from with no orphan calls; the final rate card emerges only from the assembled chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon data-merge production task: 14 connector calls across 3 tool groups (asset_* stock/storage/preview, image_* raster prep, document_* InDesign merge+export), 10 distinct Adobe tools — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from correctly staging a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge: sourcing+licensing a legibility-safe background, vectorizing a logo for print, trimming a supplied compliance artwork, running TWO separate upload/finalize sessions (placement assets vs template+data) and organizing them into a project folder before binding a real authored .indd against a regulated premium CSV, then dual-exporting press PDF + high-res JPEG and proofing the result. It respects every hard limit: the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout binds and document_render_layout only EXPORTS the client's authored .indd, which is a supplied input; no generative fill / outpaint / text-to-image / from-scratch art / upscaling / object-removal; image_fill_area is unused; the disclosure text is placed as a supplied image (no text authoring claimed); premium values are bound verbatim from the CSV per the brief.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capabilities used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo (the wordmark and disclosure are supplied raster inputs — only vectorized/cropped, not generated); no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design (fonts come from the client's brand-locked .indd, not authored here); no unreachable external accounts. image_fill_area is NOT used. document_merge_data_layout is [T] and document_render_layout only exports — neither composes a layout; the .indd is a declared supplied authored input (kind:data, gen_model:'n/a-authored-asset').
reverify.notes: All 14 steps use real Adobe-connector tools (server=='adobe'); every risky step stays in-bounds: image_vectorize (n4) is raster->vector of a supplied flat PNG, image_crop_to_bounds (n5) only trims an existing image, the [T] data-merge (n11) binds a supplied authored .indd whose real <<fields>> match the CSV headers exactly, document_render_layout (n12/n13) only exports, and asset_inline_preview (n3/n14) is inspect-only. Only IP fix applied: the client brand 'Northlantic Insurance Group' collided with the real Northlantic Insurance Company (a Rhode Island carrier), so it was renamed to the clearly-invented, non-colliding fictional brand 'Northlantic Insurance Group', with the wordmark glyph changed from a lighthouse-Northlantic to a compass-rose/north-star to stay coherent with the new name and the 'Northlantic Slate' color renamed to 'Northlantic Slate'. Adobe/Adobe Stock are correctly retained. No connector logic, exec modes, tool set, or gen-free posture changed.
